---
layout: post
title: "Lloyd George Mieyette (CECCCA)"
date: 2022-08-13 18:54:00 +0000
tags:
  - "Lloyd Mieyette"
  - "Operation Garlic"
  - "Lloyd George Mieyette"
published: true
---

<p><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></p><h1 class="userCardTitle"><span style="font-size: medium;"><span style="font-weight: normal;">Lloyd George Mieyette (</span></span><span style="font-weight: normal;"><span style="font-size: medium;"><span face="&quot;Arial&quot;, sans-serif" style="letter-spacing: -0.1pt;">CECCCA</span>)</span></span><span style="font-size: medium;"><span style="font-weight: normal;"> was born July 5, 1921 in Melville, Saskatchewan, Canada to Paul Ferdinand MIEYETTE and Mable Ann GILBANK.&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span></h1><h1 class="userCardTitle"><span style="font-size: medium;"><span style="font-weight: normal;">Lloyd joined the Royal Air Force in 1941.</span></span></h1><p><span style="font-size: medium;">He was a member of the 617 Squadron.&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"></span></p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhHTbSvPltNGsBtkH7VYl8ny-RuK4nR7L9jUXempp0b1PYeuCeT_2KIgD5rSAWIzG8w5XzoQ2mhWzVPtOmT474ZVc3ZsGdEvAkji0YBfioihs3G6FKYDBYGGYDqQTHv5K20g3y5-AuOMKufGhWwVdQtVr4W9x9pSKSnuxZDMERtGjE9VC6FLOAAHv3rgw/s800/large_000000.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="629" data-original-width="800" height="315" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhHTbSvPltNGsBtkH7VYl8ny-RuK4nR7L9jUXempp0b1PYeuCeT_2KIgD5rSAWIzG8w5XzoQ2mhWzVPtOmT474ZVc3ZsGdEvAkji0YBfioihs3G6FKYDBYGGYDqQTHv5K20g3y5-AuOMKufGhWwVdQtVr4W9x9pSKSnuxZDMERtGjE9VC6FLOAAHv3rgw/w400-h315/large_000000.jpg" width="400" /></a></div><br /><p></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">Left to right: Flight Sergeant Trevor H 
Payne, front gunner; Pilot Officer Thomas W Johnson, flight engineer; 
Sergeant Eric Hornby, rear gunner; <span style="background-color: #fcff01;">Sergeant Lloyd G Mieyette, wireless 
operator</span>; Pilot Officer George H Coles, bomb-aimer; Flying Officer James
 A Rodger, navigator; and Flight Lieutenant Harold S Wilson. All were 
killed when their Lancaster was shot down on the night of 15 /16 
September 1943 during the raid on the Dortmund-Ems Canal. Credit: https://www.iwm.org.uk/collections/item/object/205123899</span></i></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">According to the Royal Air Force's website, the 617 Squadron was formed in 1943 "...at RAF Scampton, specifically for an attack on dams in the
 Ruhr Valley using the Barnes Wallis-designed Upkeep 'bouncing bomb'."1</span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">During
 his time in the service, Lloyd was a Wireless Operator/Air Gunner.&nbsp; As I can determine, he operated one of the machine gun turrets on an airplane in a group
 called Dam Busters. Their job, as the name suggests, was to drop bombs 
on dams to destroy them.&nbsp; On March 16, 1942 he was awarded the Air 
Gunners Badge.&nbsp; I'm getting a hint of a suggestion he was the radio man.&nbsp; I'm unclear on exactly what this role's responsibility was.<br /></span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">Lloyd would have flown in the Avro Lancaster heavy bomber.</span></p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEjeQek8axxUc7cHjS8OSWjFyzn_EZEaEKOj470211SLyff8fidKQqvufJLDQooSvmTNUmyXsbs9CRqTbINGApEqOH-wuOsS2cbLmumY43g-8K0Xirgael1vVY8R-JF-sY_A251IzX03c1Zh-jp0UaHXXQX_9sU7WcikbMYsMaS1IGC9DL2IbxR_blYm5Q/s1024/bomber-Lancaster-World-War-II-Royal-Air.webp" imageanchor="1" style="clear: left; float: left; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="751" data-original-width="1024" height="294" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEjeQek8axxUc7cHjS8OSWjFyzn_EZEaEKOj470211SLyff8fidKQqvufJLDQooSvmTNUmyXsbs9CRqTbINGApEqOH-wuOsS2cbLmumY43g-8K0Xirgael1vVY8R-JF-sY_A251IzX03c1Zh-jp0UaHXXQX_9sU7WcikbMYsMaS1IGC9DL2IbxR_blYm5Q/w400-h294/bomber-Lancaster-World-War-II-Royal-Air.webp" width="400" /></a></div><br /><p><br />&nbsp;<i><span style="font-size: small;"> <br /></span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></i></p><p><i><span style="font-size: small;">Credit: Britannica,&nbsp; https://www.britannica.com/technology/Lancaster-airplane#/media/1/691874/101027</span></i></p><h1 class="userCardTitle"></h1><h1 class="userCardTitle"><span style="font-size: medium;"><span style="font-weight: normal;">Lloyd enjoyed rapid promotions.&nbsp; Taken from his Service and Pay Book,&nbsp;</span></span></h1><h1 class="userCardTitle"></h1><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEigyqYakHe5FMryRflpr141q_KxAIXoL7BC7MEiipYQ27rrM0__wWRn_S_jCIwbMDEeKpjRp9MxjiS4xN0EZRLPT9PgMbH6eI1Z2kQgvRbDYArkaclhw_vCup46G2LC1Ber31GVUaxxR2Ru0aFD_mr64JdjrnO1I9rtFtESSWiR4U2CayGLfN3ZGFaXbA/s416/Ranks.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="clear: left; float: left; margin-bottom: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="281" data-original-width="416" height="216" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEigyqYakHe5FMryRflpr141q_KxAIXoL7BC7MEiipYQ27rrM0__wWRn_S_jCIwbMDEeKpjRp9MxjiS4xN0EZRLPT9PgMbH6eI1Z2kQgvRbDYArkaclhw_vCup46G2LC1Ber31GVUaxxR2Ru0aFD_mr64JdjrnO1I9rtFtESSWiR4U2CayGLfN3ZGFaXbA/s320/Ranks.jpg" width="320" /></a></div><br /><p><br /></p><p></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">Operation Garlic <br /></span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">After a previous failed attempt to destroy the Dortmund-EMS Canal in Germany (Operation Chastise), Canada launched Operation Garlic during the days of September 14-16, 1943. </span></p><p><span style="font-size: medium;">The raid began on the early morning of September 15, 1943.&nbsp; Aircraft were recalled due to fog and mist over the target.&nbsp; The next morning, September 16, 1943, the raid flew again at the target.&nbsp; There isn't positive agreement on what went wrong.&nbsp; Again weather caused difficulties with target identification and the planes were being shot at.&nbsp; Flight Lieutenant Wilson, the pilot of the aircraft Lloyd was on, requested permission to attack, and no further communication occurred with his aircraft.</span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;"><span style="font-size: medium;">Lloyd was officially listed as killed in action, September 16, 1943.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;<img alt="" height="64" src="data:image/png;base64," width="640" /></span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: small;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhTer8wfk-c4hK3p46JUgDMgP6sgGg-lJzm_-OavPdZzIrvt9fNUTxA-j27hlGlcRE47c6cLmrAI0_B3z6Xj4rjeTlSfS85z2TTj2OONlKjlvGDNGEfABg4rvatDgnH1CEF0NZ_9Ya6d2_8vkcHk82rg9faxViTvoXet_rMZVSvmmCc1RNA4QVH_7uQSA/s4000/Lloyd%20George%20Mieyette%20-%20Headstone.jpg" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="4000" data-original-width="3000" height="640" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEhTer8wfk-c4hK3p46JUgDMgP6sgGg-lJzm_-OavPdZzIrvt9fNUTxA-j27hlGlcRE47c6cLmrAI0_B3z6Xj4rjeTlSfS85z2TTj2OONlKjlvGDNGEfABg4rvatDgnH1CEF0NZ_9Ya6d2_8vkcHk82rg9faxViTvoXet_rMZVSvmmCc1RNA4QVH_7uQSA/w480-h640/Lloyd%20George%20Mieyette%20-%20Headstone.jpg" width="480" /></a></span></div><span style="font-size: small;"><br /></span><p></p><p><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></p><div abp="38" style="font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot;; text-align: center;"><div abp="883">~ Tom ~</div></div><center abp="39" style="font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot;;"><i abp="40" style="font-family: &quot;Book Antiqua&quot;;">CADECBAB</i></center><center abp="39" style="font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot;;"><i style="font-family: &quot;book antiqua&quot;; text-align: left;">8 August 2022</i></center><p><span style="font-size: small;">&nbsp;</span></p><p><span style="font-size: small;">1. https://www.raf.mod.uk/our-organisation/squadrons/617-squadron/ </span><br /></p>
